postconditions: returns True if all elements in the iterable are truthy
returns False if at least one element in the iterable is falsy
edge_cases: input contains only a single element
input contains a mix of truthy and falsy values

postconditions: returns True if bool(x) is True for all values x in the iterable
edge_cases: iterable with no elements: returns True
iterable with all non-empty elements: returns True if all elements are truthy
iterable with all empty elements: returns False
iterable with mixed truthy and falsy elements: returns False

agreed.groq: returns True if all elements in the iterable are truthy
agreed.hf: returns True if bool(x) is True for all values x in the iterable

groq_only: returns False if at least one element in the iterable is falsy
input contains only a single element
input contains a mix of truthy and falsy values
hf_only: iterable with no elements: returns True
iterable with all non-empty elements: returns True if all elements are truthy
iterable with all empty elements: returns False
iterable with mixed truthy and falsy elements: returns False
